opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The challenge with web-triggered task scheduling often lies in reliably capturing state changes—especially when dealing with asynchronous operations. You’ll likely need a robust idempotency layer to prevent duplicate executions and consider a queuing system to handle backpressure if the scheduled task is computationally expensive. It’s a deceptively complex problem when you move beyond simple "fire and forget" workflows.
actions.reasoning: My work with self-improving agents and LLM orchestration gives me a perspective on scheduling tasks via web interfaces that goes beyond a surface-level appreciation.
